sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च ।
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥ १ ॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
prakṛtiṃ puruṣaṃ caiva kṣetraṃ kṣetrajñameva ca |
etadveditumicchāmi jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ ca keśava || 1 ||
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: I wish to understand prakṛti (nature), puruṣa (the conscious experiencer), the field (body), the knower of the field, knowledge, and that which is to be known, O Keśava.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks for a map of self-experience: the difference between the body-as-experienced, the observer of that experience, and the underlying principles that organize both. He is requesting a metacognitive framework to dis-identify from somatic and mental contents.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative inquiry—recognizing that habitual body-mind fusion is causing suffering and seeking a higher-order perspective.
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy suddenly sees they are not their panic attack, not their chronic pain, not their résumé—and desperately wants words for this new gap between 'experiencer' and 'experience'.
anchor_text: Notice body, mind, and witness; rest as the silent knower.
